title: Charred Polish Paella with Rice Stick Noodles
description: Charred, crisp kid-friendly Polish dish featuring rice stick noodles, ideal for a quick dinner. Perfect for a cozy evening with family and friends.
instructions: Preheat the paella pan over medium-high heat, about 3-4 minutes
Cook the sliced onions and minced garlic until the onions are translucent, about 5 minutes
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 7 minutes
Remove the chicken and set aside
Add the sliced Polish sausage to the pan and cook for 2 minutes
Add the chopped onions and minced garlic back into the pan, cook for 2 minutes
Add the uncooked short-grain rice to the pan and stir to coat the rice in the oil for about 2 minutes
Add the paprika, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper to the pan, stir for 1 minute
Add the rinsed and drained rice mixture to the paella pan, stirring to avoid any burning, about 3-4 minutes
Add the chicken broth, lemon juice, and tomato paste to the pan, stir to combine, about 2 minutes
Add the cooked chicken, sliced Polish sausage, and chopped tomatoes to the pan, stir to combine, about 2 minutes
Reduce heat to low and gently simmer for about 25 minutes, without stirring, until the liquid has evaporated and the rice is cooked
Stir in the beaten eggs, if using
Transfer the paella to a serving plate, sprinkle with chopped parsley and dill, and serve
Garnish with additional parsley and dill, if desired
